name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 58

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written (Ground level 61.85 m O D)

units: metres; typed site-investigation log, depth = base of interval below ground; Reduced Level (O D Level, m O D) column cross-checked against 61.85 m O D datum

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL (dark brown slightly clayey sandy silt with occasional fine angular to subrounded gravel and rootlets)
Loose to medium dense light brown and dark brown silty fine and medium SAND with rootlets
Medium dense orange brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional coarse sand and fine angular fragments of sandstone
annotations: water: Remarks record a slight seepage of ground-water at 2.00m; 'GWe' marked in the Water depth column at 2.10m; hole otherwise noted DRY

intervals.verbatim: Orange brown fine and medium grained SANDSTONE
annotations: base of cable-percussion boring / top of rotary core drilling at 2.55m
OD tick at base reads 59.35 (= 61.85-2.50); 61.85-2.55 = 59.30, a ~0.05m clerk/rounding offset

intervals.verbatim: Orangeish grey fine grained moderately and highly discoloured slightly to moderately weathered thinly laminated SANDSTONE - strong and moderately strong. Laminae are dark grey silty and rippled down to 3.50m, becoming planar and less apparent towards base. Closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures and some 60 degree to subvertical dark reddish brown ironstained fractures
annotations: core-run boundary at 3.90m within

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained slightly weathered thinly ripple laminated SANDSTONE - strong. Occasional subhorizontal bedding plane fractures and 70 degree to subvertical dark reddish brown ironstained fractures
Dark grey fine grained highly weathered thinly laminated fissile SILTSTONE - very weak with some moderately weak fragments. Extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures
Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Many carbonaceous traces throughout. Medium spaced subhorizontal to 20 degree bedding plane fractures
annotations: core-run boundary at 6.50m within

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine and medium to coarse grained slightly discoloured slightly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Many carbonaceous traces and thin laminae of coal. Closely and close to medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some subvertical dark reddish brown ironstained fractures with pervasive orange discolouration
annotations: thin laminae of coal noted within (not a mappable seam)

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained slightly weathered thinly laminated micaceous silty SANDSTONE - strong. Many thin predominantly planar carbonaceous laminae. Occasional bands up to 100mm thick with slumped bedding and a concentration of thicker (up to 3mm) laminae of coal. Closely and medium spaced 10 degree to 20 degree bedding plane fractures. Some subvertical orange ironstained fractures
annotations: thicker coal laminae (up to 3mm) within slumped bands
core-run boundary at 9.50m within

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL - very weak. Bed of highly fractured thinly laminated siltstone from 9.80m to 10.00m
annotations: contains a bed of highly fractured thinly laminated siltstone 9.80-10.00m
seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation marked

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained completely becoming highly weathered silty carbonaceous MUDSTONE - very weak

Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered thinly ripple laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Beds of dark grey thinly planar laminated fissile siltstone (moderately strong) from 13.25m to 14.00m and 15.00m to 15.15m. Bed of highly weathered highly fractured siltstone from 15.30m to 15.50m
annotations: top taken at 11.40m (mudstone base, OD 50.45); log also marks a core-run/description top at 11.50m - a ~0.10m offset, bridged to keep continuous coverage
siltstone beds 13.25-14.00m and 15.00-15.15m; highly weathered fractured siltstone bed 15.30-15.50m

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained fresh and slightly weathered thinly laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong and strong to very strong. Dark grey silty laminae absent from 15.95m to 16.20m and 16.80m to 17.10m. Laminae are planar and dipping 5-20 degrees above 17.40m becoming rippled and subhorizontal below 17.40m. Bed of moderately to highly weathered laminated fissile highly fractured siltstone (moderately weak) from 17.20m to 17.35m. Medium to wide, occasionally very closely spaced subhorizontal to 20 degree bedding plane fractures, occasionally with orange ironstaining
annotations: fissile siltstone bed 17.20-17.35m

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately to highly weathered thinly planar laminated fissile muddy SILTSTONE - moderately weak. Extremely closely spaced subhorizontal dark orangeish brown iron-stained bedding plane fractures

Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered thinly ripple laminated sandy micaceous SILTSTONE - moderately strong to strong. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some subvertical orange iron-stained fractures. Occasional beds of planar laminated siltstone lacking the pale grey sandy laminae
annotations: unit spans sheets 2-3 (18.00-21.65m); core-run boundary at 21.50m near base
base taken at 21.65m per OD tick 40.20

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately weathered thinly planar laminated fissile highly fractured muddy SILTSTONE - moderately weak and weak. Extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Occasional thin beds lacking fissility. Many carbonaceous plant fossils
Black COAL - very weak
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation marked

intervals.verbatim: Black fine grained slightly to moderately weathered highly fractured carbonaceous MUDSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong. Many laminae of coal
annotations: many coal laminae

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL with some silty clayey bands
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation marked

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained slightly and moderately weathered thinly planar and slightly ripple laminated sandy SILTSTONE - moderately strong to strong and strong. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Moderately to highly weathered and highly fractured from 24.05m to 24.30m. Abundant carbonaceous plant fossils
modifiers: moderately strong to strong and strong
annotations: core-run boundary at 24.50m within

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered thinly planar laminated slightly fissile carbonaceous muddy SILTSTONE - moderately strong. Very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Closely spaced subvertical fractures towards base. Gradational reduction in grain size from muddy siltstone at top to silty mudstone at base. Abundant carbonaceous plant fossils
annotations: gradational base into silty mudstone

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL - very weak
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation marked

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered thinly ripple and planar laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong and moderately strong to strong. Medium and closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Moderately weathered fissile highly fractured beds from 27.85m to 27.95m, 28.40m to 28.50m and 28.50m to 28.65m
annotations: core-run boundary at 27.50m within

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately and moderately to highly weathered thinly planar laminated slightly fissile muddy SILTSTONE - moderately strong, occasionally moderately weak to moderately strong. Very and extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Very highly fractured with some clayey silt from 29.00m to 29.10m and 29.25m to 29.40m

Pale grey fine, becoming fine to medium grained fresh to slightly weathered thinly ripple laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Closely and medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Laminae occasionally show slumped bedding. Some thin beds (up to 50mm) of dark grey laminated siltstone
annotations: top taken at 29.65m continuing from sheet 3; core-run boundary at 30.50m within
END OF BOREHOLE at 32.50m (OD 29.35)

notes: Clean typed Wimpey Geotech 'BOREHOLE RECORD' (24A REV 2/88), 4 sheets, Fig. 65, scale 1:50. Boring: Cable Percussion to 2.55m, Rotary Core Drilling to 32.50m. Equipment: Dando 150; Boyles BBS20 (Lorry Mounted); 412 Core Barrel; Mist Flush. Diameters: 150mm to 2.55m then H to 32.50m; casing 150mm to 2.20m then P to 2.50m. Orientation Vertical. Local location grid 5712.5E/1543.3N. Date commenced 28/3/90; originator DJM/AMcL 28/3/90 (sheet 1) & 17/4/90; checked/approved AMcL 28/6/90. Ground level 61.85 m O D. Depths are base-of-interval below ground; O D (Reduced) Levels checked against the 61.85 datum and used to fix contacts. Remarks (sheet 4 verbatim): 'A slight seepage of ground-water was encountered at a depth of 2.00m. The borehole was advanced by chiselling from 2.10m to 2.50m (1h). Full return of circulation medium during rotary drilling. The borehole was backfilled with bentonite/cement grout.' Four thin COAL bands logged (10, 22.85, 23.6, 25.6m approx) plus two carbonaceous mudstones - all UNNAMED in the original, no pencil seam correlations present, so seam fields left null and seam_marks empty. Minor arithmetic/label offsets noted per interval: base of interval 4 (2.55m) carries OD 59.35 (=61.85-2.50, ~0.05m off); interval 12/13 contact marked at both 11.40m (mudstone base, OD 50.45) and 11.50m (core-run/description top), ~0.10m offset bridged for continuity. Handwritten 'NZ24SE/213' at head of each sheet is the BGS reference, not log content; BGS blue banner, watermarks and 'No S/ 26290' stamp ignored. Total depth 32.50m matches index depth. No location plan or key sheet among the four pages.
